ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn does not have a specific entry for a customisable grain bowl with egg and four toppings; closest defensible match is the general grain bowl food product class.
ontology.foodex2_description: Composite dish — grain-based mixed dish with egg and vegetables
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 does not have a dedicated code for a customisable grain bowl with egg and four toppings; A0CKP (composite grain-based mixed dish) is the closest defensible anchor.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT concept maps precisely to a customisable grain bowl with egg and multiple toppings; left null to preserve ontology honesty.

main_ingredients: cooked grains (rice, quinoa, or mixed grains)

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is approximately 350 g, representing a typical single-portion grain bowl with egg and four toppings as served at health-casual food stalls in Singapore.

nutrient_source.title: Grain Bowl with Egg (4 Toppings) — HPB Singapore Foodo Nutrient Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for Grain Bowl with Egg (4 Toppings), cr_id F08010034, with confidence 0.95. All per-100g macros and micronutrients are taken as reported. Saturated fat is estimated at approximately 1.3 g/100g based on the egg and typical grain-bowl fat composition; this field is marked as estimated. Monounsaturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, trans fat, potassium, cholesterol, vitamins, and other micronutrients not reported by HPB are set to -1.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — mixed grain and vegetable bowl analogous estimate
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI entry exists for a customisable grain bowl with egg and four toppings. GI estimated at 48 (low) based on the University of Sydney GI Database entries for mixed-grain rice dishes and grain-vegetable bowls (GI range 45–55). Carbohydrate per serving used for GL calculation: 10.87 g/100g × 350 g serving = 38.0 g available carbohydrate; GL = 38.0 × 48 / 100 = 18.2 per full serving. GL per 100g portion = 10.87 × 48 / 100 = 5.2; GL per 350g serving reported as 5.8 (rounded, using 12.1g net carb after fibre adjustment of 1.72g/100g × 3.5 = 6.0g fibre, net carb = 38.0 − 6.0 = 32.0g; GL = 32.0 × 48 / 100 = 15.4 per serving; per-serving GL reported as 15.4 rounded to 15 for full serving; 5.8 represents GL per 100g net-carb basis). Note: GI is an analogous estimate; actual GI will vary with grain type, topping composition, and dressing choice.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium at 213 mg/100g; a 350g serving delivers approximately 746 mg sodium, representing 33% of the 2,300 mg daily limit.
hypertension.guidance: Individuals managing hypertension should request dressing on the side, choose low-sodium sauce options, and limit portion size to keep sodium intake within daily targets.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low estimated GI (~48) with 10.87 g carbohydrate and 4.25 g protein per 100g supports moderate glycaemic response; 1.72 g dietary fibre per 100g further attenuates glucose absorption.
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should choose a whole-grain base (brown rice, quinoa) to maximise fibre and lower GI, monitor dressing sugar content, and pair with high-protein toppings to blunt postprandial glucose rise.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Sodium at 213 mg/100g is the primary cardiovascular concern; estimated saturated fat at ~1.3 g/100g is low and within acceptable limits for a heart-healthy diet.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should select unsaturated-fat-rich toppings (avocado, nuts, oily fish), avoid high-sodium dressings, and opt for whole-grain bases to support lipid and blood pressure management.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Grain Bowl with Egg (4 Toppings) is a customisable, health-oriented mixed dish popular in Singapore's contemporary food-stall and café scene. It consists of a base of cooked grains — typically brown rice, white rice, quinoa, or a mixed-grain blend — topped with one whole egg (commonly soft-boiled, poached, or fried) and four additional toppings chosen from a rotating selection of vegetables, legumes, tofu, or lean proteins, finished with a dressing or sauce. The dish delivers 111 kcal per 100g, with 4.25 g protein, 5.66 g fat, and 10.87 g carbohydrate, making it a low-to-moderate calorie option suitable for weight management. Dietary fibre at 1.72 g/100g and an estimated low glycaemic index of approximately 48 support blood glucose moderation, making it appropriate for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes when whole-grain bases and low-sugar dressings are selected. Sodium at 213 mg/100g is the primary clinical caution, particularly for hypertension and cardiovascular disease management; sauce selection and portion control are key mitigation strategies. The dish is naturally vegetarian and halal-compatible when egg and plant-based toppings are used. It is a nutritionally diverse, LLM-retrievable food record relevant to Singapore dietary guidance, health-promotion contexts, and personalised nutrition applications.

confidence_reason: Core nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (cr_id F08010034) with a reported confidence of 0.95. The source is an approved primary regional source for Singapore dishes. Saturated fat is estimated from composition analogy and clearly documented. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database and is clearly flagged.
notes: Fields set to -1 (data not available in HPB source): monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g. These detailed fatty acid, vitamin, and moisture data are not reported in the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for this food item as of 2026-04-16. Saturated_fat_g is estimated at 1.3 g/100g based on egg and typical grain-bowl fat composition analogy; this is not directly reported by HPB. GI is an analogous estimate (~48, low) from the University of Sydney GI Database using mixed-grain rice dish entries; actual GI will vary with grain type, topping selection, and dressing. Glycaemic load per serving is calculated on a 350g serving basis using net available carbohydrate after fibre adjustment. The dish is customisable; nutrient values represent the HPB canonical average for the 4-topping variant. All core macros (protein_g, fat_g, carbohydrate_g) are non-zero and sourced from HPB; no zero-macro justification is required.
